@eshear @nickcammarata Given what we know about quantum mechanics, I'm not sure the discrete nature of reality does imply binaries. "Is there a particle in this Planck volume?" is still going to be, from our perspective, non-deterministic.
@eshear @nickcammarata We're reaching the limits of my physics knowledge, but as I understand it only the wave function is fundamental, and space and particles are abstractions/descriptions of it.

@eshear @nickcammarata Sometimes! But entanglement is the normal case. Also, to be clear, I'm just questioning the specific assertion that minimum resolution implies binaries. I generally think binary distinctions are useful even if close inspection reveals fuzzy boundaries.

-GPT 4o
-GPT 4 level intelligence but faster and cheaper
-Free! (pay for 5x capacity)
-Voice and vision updates, see next tweet
-Available in ChatGPT and API
-Desktop app (esp. useful for coding)
-Changes rolling out over next few weeks

Voice updates:
-Less lag before response
-Can be interrupted
-Speaks with and understands emotion 
-Demo application: real time two-way translation

Vision updates: 
-Process camera feed in real time
-Understand emotion on faces

@tradegal_ (voted yes|yes) As I understand it, the goal of the pricing structure is to encourage quick commitment from buyers, so that you have time to respond to demand (by e.g. building more space). Scalpers benefit you by taking risk off of your hands, locking in sales. 1/2

My PRNG algorithm: Seed with the number of seconds in the current time (treat 0 as 60). Take the tens place times 3 and the ones place times 7, and add those together. Yield the ones digit of the sum. Repeat using the sum as the new seed until you have as many digits as you want. https://t.co/FXfUHnXh6L

@tenobrus Working on a take for a blog post I'm writing, I'll draft it here: 

Particles, and by extension things made of them, have no persistent identities across time. You are such a structure, and should therefore consider yourself not as a particular physical body, but as the (1/?)

@tenobrus collection of patterns "sufficiently similar" to you throughout the world. The edges of that are kinda fuzzy, but probably includes the person with your name tomorrow and the person with your name yesterday. Assuming the existence of a multiverse, which I argue for (2/?)

@tenobrus elsewhere in the post, your identity should include all your copies (the set of observers with indistinguishable experience) throughout the multiverse, rather than attaching to a particular instance of you in a particular universe. (3/3)
